title: larrea divaricata var. arenaria
intro: Larrea divaricata var. arenaria, also known as creosote bush, is a shrub that belongs to the Zygophyllaceae family and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat a variety of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. In terms of herbal preparations, creosote bush can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
